actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

dinosaurs.name: Acrocanthosaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee +9 to hit. Hit: 25 (3d12+6) piercing.

historical_facts: Name means 'high-spined lizard'.
Had a ridge of high spines along its neck and back.
Was the apex predator of its time in North America.

dinosaurs.name: Adasaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +1 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A small dinosaur from the Mesozoic Era.

actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +-2 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A tiny, agile dinosaur.
Likely ate insects and small lizards.
Used its small size to hide from larger predators.

dinosaurs.name: Afrovenator

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A medium dinosaur from the Mesozoic Era.
Well-adapted to its specific environment.
Contributed to the diverse ecosystem of its time.

dinosaurs.name: Alamosaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +13 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: One of the last sauropods to ever exist.
Named after the Ojo Alamo Formation in New Mexico.
Could reach weights of up to 80 tons.

dinosaurs.name: Albertosaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A smaller, faster relative of the T-Rex.
Named after Alberta, Canada, where it was discovered.
Evidence suggests they may have hunted in packs.

dinosaurs.name: Albinykus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +-2 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A tiny, agile dinosaur.
Likely ate insects and small lizards.

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A large dinosaur from the Mesozoic Era.
Well-adapted to its specific environment.
Contributed to the diverse ecosystem of its time.

dinosaurs.name: Allosaurus

traits.name: Pounce
traits.description: Charge, hit, knock prone, bonus bite.

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee +6 to hit. Hit: 15 (2d10+4) piercing.

historical_facts: Most common large predator of the Jurassic.
Had crests in front of its eyes.
Name means 'different lizard'.

dinosaurs.name: Amargasaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: Famous for the double row of long spines on its neck.
A relatively small sauropod from South America.
The spines may have supported a skin sail or been bare.

dinosaurs.name: Ankylosaurus

actions.name: Tail
actions.description: Melee +7 to hit. Hit: 18 (4d6+4) bludgeoning. DC 14 Str or prone.

historical_facts: Known as the 'living tank'.
Had a massive bony club at the end of its tail.
Even its eyelids were armored.

dinosaurs.name: Anserimimus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A medium dinosaur from the Mesozoic Era.
Well-adapted to its specific environment.
Contributed to the diverse ecosystem of its time.

dinosaurs.name: Apatosaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: Name means 'deceptive lizard'.
Once thought to be the same as Brontosaurus.
Had a thick, powerful neck and a long whip-tail.

dinosaurs.name: Archaeopteryx

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee +4 to hit. Hit: 1 piercing damage.

historical_facts: Often called the 'first bird'.
Had both bird-like features (feathers) and dinosaur-like features (teeth, long tail).
Lived in the Late Jurassic.

dinosaurs.name: Archelon

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A large dinosaur from the Mesozoic Era.
Well-adapted to its specific environment.
Contributed to the diverse ecosystem of its time.

dinosaurs.name: Argentinosaurus

traits.name: Siege Monster
traits.description: Deals double damage to objects and structures.

actions.name: Stomp
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +14 to hit. Hit: 45 (6d10 + 12) bludgeoning damage. DC 18 Str save or prone.

historical_facts: One of the largest known land animals to ever exist.
A single vertebra was over 5 feet tall.
Estimated to weigh up to 100 tons.

dinosaurs.name: Atrociraptor

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +1 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A small dinosaur from the Mesozoic Era.

dinosaurs.name: Australovenator

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: Nicknamed 'Banjo' after an Australian poet.
Built like a cheetah for high-speed pursuits.
The most complete meat-eating dinosaur from Australia.

dinosaurs.name: Austroposeidon

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +13 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A massive gargantuan herbivore.
Lived during the golden age of dinosaurs.
Fossils have been found in multiple continents.

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +1 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A massive huge herbivore.

historical_facts: Name means 'heavy claw'.
Specialized in eating fish, like a modern heron.
Had a crocodile-like snout.

dinosaurs.name: Basilosaurus

actions.description: Melee +11 to hit. Hit: 34 (5d10 + 7) bludgeoning damage.

historical_facts: Its front legs were longer than its back legs.
Had a very high neck, allowing it to reach treetops.
One of the few dinosaurs that could have eaten from 40 feet up.

dinosaurs.name: Brontosaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: Name means 'thunder lizard'.
Long considered the same as Apatosaurus, but now a separate genus.
Could weigh up to 30 tons.

dinosaurs.name: Bruhathkayosaurus

actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +13 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: Name means 'chambered lizard' due to hollows in its vertebrae.
Had a blunt, boxy skull and strong, spoon-shaped teeth.
One of the most common sauropods in the Morrison Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Carbonemys

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

actions.description: Melee +11 to hit. Hit: 35 (5d10+8) piercing.

historical_facts: Name means 'shark-toothed lizard'.
Had teeth up to 8 inches long.
Lived in North Africa alongside Spinosaurus.

dinosaurs.name: Carnotaurus

traits.name: Charge
traits.description: Moves 20ft, hits with horns for extra 2d6 and knock prone.

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee +6 to hit. Hit: 15 (2d10+4) piercing.

actions.name: Horns
actions.description: Melee +6 to hit. Hit: 11 (2d6+4) piercing.

historical_facts: Name means 'meat-eating bull' because of its horns.
Had incredibly short arms, even smaller than T-Rex.
Was built for high-speed sprinting in straight lines.

dinosaurs.name: Ceratonykus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +-2 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: Known for the large horn on its snout.
Had a row of bony plates (osteoderms) along its back.
Possessed exceptionally long, blade-like teeth.

dinosaurs.name: Changyuraptor

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +1 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A small dinosaur from the Mesozoic Era.

dinosaurs.name: Chromogisaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +-2 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A tiny, agile dinosaur.

Used its small size to hide from larger predators.

dinosaurs.name: Coelophysis

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A lean, agile hunter from the Late Triassic.
One of the most common early dinosaurs.
Had hollow bones to reduce weight for speed.

dinosaurs.name: Compsognathus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee +5 to hit. Hit: 1 piercing damage.

historical_facts: For a long time, it was the smallest known dinosaur.
About the size of a modern chicken.
Lived in what is now Europe.

dinosaurs.name: Corythosaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: Had a hollow, helmet-like crest on its head.
Used its crest to make loud, resonating sounds.
Known from exceptionally well-preserved fossils.

dinosaurs.name: Cryolophosaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: Nicknamed 'Elvisaurus' due to its pompadour-like crest.
The first predatory dinosaur found in Antarctica.
Lived during the Early Jurassic.

dinosaurs.name: Daspletosaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A large dinosaur from the Mesozoic Era.
Well-adapted to its specific environment.
Contributed to the diverse ecosystem of its time.

dinosaurs.name: Deinocheirus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: Name means 'terrible hand' due to its 8-foot-long arms.
Had a duck-like beak and a large hump on its back.
Was an omnivore that ate both plants and small animals.

dinosaurs.name: Deinonychus

traits.name: Pounce
traits.description: Charge, hit, knock prone, bonus bite.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.description: Makes three attacks: one bite and two claws.

historical_facts: Name means 'terrible claw'.
The dinosaur that inspired the 'raptors' in Jurassic Park.
Provided the first evidence that dinosaurs were active, warm-blooded animals.

dinosaurs.name: Deinosuchus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A massive huge herbivore.

dinosaurs.name: Dilophosaurus

actions.description: Melee +5 to hit. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) piercing damage.

historical_facts: There is no evidence they could spit venom or had neck frills.

Had two distinctive crests on its head.

actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: One of the longest dinosaurs, reaching up to 90 feet.
Had a whip-like tail that could possibly break the sound barrier.
Lived in North America during the Late Jurassic.

dinosaurs.name: Dreadnoughtus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +13 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: Name means 'oak lizard'.
Had a horny beak for cropping plants.
Relied on speed and camouflage for survival.

dinosaurs.name: Edmontosaurus

actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

Used its neck to ambush schools of fish.
Was first reconstructed with its head on its tail by mistake.

actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +-2 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A medium dinosaur from the Mesozoic Era.
Well-adapted to its specific environment.
Contributed to the diverse ecosystem of its time.

dinosaurs.name: Suchomimus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: Name means 'crocodile mimic' due to its long snout.
Specialized in catching fish with its large thumb claws.
Had a small sail on its back.

dinosaurs.name: Supersaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +13 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A massive gargantuan herbivore.
Lived during the golden age of dinosaurs.
Fossils have been found in multiple continents.

dinosaurs.name: Tapejara

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +1 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A small dinosaur from the Mesozoic Era.
Well-adapted to its specific environment.
Contributed to the diverse ecosystem of its time.

dinosaurs.name: Tarbosaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: The apex predator of Late Cretaceous Asia.
Very closely related to T-Rex but with smaller arms.
Its skull was more rigid than T-Rex's.

dinosaurs.name: Therizinosaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: Possessed the longest claws of any known animal, up to 3 feet long.
Despite its terrifying look, it was a herbivore.
Lived in Mongolia during the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Thescelosaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +1 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A heavily built runner from the Late Cretaceous.
Found with a structure once thought to be a fossilized heart.
One of the last dinosaurs to exist before the extinction.

dinosaurs.name: Titanoboa

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A massive huge herbivore.
Lived during the golden age of dinosaurs.
Fossils have been found in multiple continents.

dinosaurs.name: Torvosaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A large dinosaur from the Mesozoic Era.
Well-adapted to its specific environment.
Contributed to the diverse ecosystem of its time.

dinosaurs.name: Triceratops

traits.name: Trampling Charge
traits.description: Moves 20ft and hits to potentially knock prone and stomp.

actions.name: Gore
actions.description: Melee +9 to hit. Hit: 24 (4d8+6) piercing.

historical_facts: Name means 'three-horned face'.
Lived alongside T-Rex.
Had hundreds of teeth in batteries.

dinosaurs.name: Troodon

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee +5 to hit. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) piercing damage.

historical_facts: Considered the most intelligent dinosaur based on brain-to-body size.
Had large, forward-facing eyes for nocturnal hunting.
Had serrated teeth like a saw.

dinosaurs.name: Tsaagan

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +1 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A small dinosaur from the Mesozoic Era.
Well-adapted to its specific environment.

dinosaurs.name: Tyrannosaurus Rex

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit. Hit: 33 (4d12 + 7) piercing damage. Target is grappled (DC 17).

actions.name: Tail
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit. Hit: 20 (3d8 + 7) bludgeoning damage.

historical_facts: Strongest bite force of any land animal.
Excellent binocular vision.
Lived in the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Tyrannotitan

dinosaurs.name: Utahraptor

traits.name: Pounce
traits.description: Move 20ft, hit, knock prone, bonus bite.

actions.name: Claw
actions.description: Melee +6 to hit. Hit: 13 (2d8+4) slashing.

historical_facts: The largest 'raptor' (dromaeosaurid) ever discovered.
Had a 9-inch long killing claw on its second toe.
Was much bulkier than portrayed in movies.

dinosaurs.name: Velociraptor

traits.name: Pack Tactics
traits.description: Advantage on attacks if an ally is within 5ft.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.description: Makes one bite and one claw attack.

historical_facts: Real velociraptors were about the size of a turkey.
They were covered in feathers.
Used their sickle-shaped claw to puncture vital organs.

dinosaurs.name: Yangchuanosaurus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A large dinosaur from the Mesozoic Era.
Well-adapted to its specific environment.

dinosaurs.name: Yi Qi

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +-2 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A tiny, agile dinosaur.
Likely ate insects and small lizards.
Used its small size to hide from larger predators.

dinosaurs.name: Yutyrannus

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +13 to hit. Hit: 10 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: The largest dinosaur found with direct evidence of feathers.
Name means 'feathered tyrant'.
Lived in a cooler climate in what is now China.
